description:
Advances: none (test infrastructure only — no spec subject changes behavior)

Deliverable:
Root-cause fix for the slow merge gate that motivated specled_-s65.3. Investigation (session bbf3b419, 2026-07-02) found the tagged_tests verification command is slow because the test suite inherits the developer's global git config: commit.gpgsign=true + gpg.format=ssh + gpg.ssh.program=/Applications/1Password.app/Contents/MacOS/op-ssh-sign means every git commit the tests make in temp fixture repos does IPC with the 1Password app. Measured: 2.54s per signed commit vs 0.30s unsigned; full suite 366s with global config vs 174s with GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL=/dev/null (mix test --include integration, 816 tests, same host). The 59 verification_command_failed findings and the s65.3 timeout bump were symptoms of this leak, not of a slow host.

Fix: make the suite hermetic to host git config. In test/test_helper.exs, before ExUnit.start():
- System.put_env("GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL", "/dev/null")
- System.put_env("GIT_CONFIG_NOSYSTEM", "1")
Env vars propagate to every child process the test BEAM spawns — all SpecLedEx.Case.git!/2 calls, the direct git show in branch_check_test.exs, lib code's read-only git calls during tests, and the child mix runs from SpecLedEx.IntegrationCase. No per-callsite changes needed: init_git_repo/1 already sets local user.name/user.email, so nothing from the global config is required. Add a short comment explaining why (host commit-signing config must not leak into fixture commits).

Verification:
- Tests to pass: full suite (mix test --include integration) green, 0 failures.
- Behaviors to demonstrate: suite wall time drops roughly in half on a host with 1Password commit-signing configured (~366s -> ~174s reference measurement); no test asserts on signed commits.
- Commands: mix compile --warnings-as-errors && mix test && mix format --check-formatted && mix deps.unlock --check-unused && mix spec.check

Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- test/test_helper.exs
- .spec/state.json (regenerated only, if spec.check rewrites it)

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/**
- test_support/**
- test/** other than test_helper.exs
- .spec/specs/**
- .spec/config.yml

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do not revisit the s65.3 command_timeout_ms value here (follow-up: with this fix the merged run is ~170s, so 400s becomes comfortable headroom; a later ticket may lower it to ~240s).
- Do not attempt the larger levers found in the same investigation (unglobalize Mix.shell to unlock async: false -> async: true on 33 files; double suite execution in the merge gate; excluding :integration from plain mix test) — candidates for separate tickets.
- No behavior change to shipped code.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly (mix compile --warnings-as-errors)
- [ ] mix test passes (full suite, --include integration)
- [ ] mix spec.check green within the configured timeout
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only test/test_helper.exs (+ regenerated .spec/state.json if applicable)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket
title: Hermetic git config in test suite — stop 1Password commit-signing from doubling suite runtime
